title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a man giving information about a farm open day.
audio_script: Speaker: Hello, everyone. I am going to tell you about the open day at Hill Farm this Saturday. It is a good chance for families to see how a working farm is run. The farmer who will meet visitors is Mrs [14]Carter[/14]. She will explain how the farm has changed since her grandparents lived there. The first guided walk begins at [15]ten thirty[/15], and there will be another one after lunch if the first is full. Please wear old shoes and bring a [16]raincoat[/16], because the weather forecast says there may be showers. The walks continue if it rains, unless the weather becomes unsafe. During the walk, children will be allowed to feed the [17]goats[/17], but only with food given by the farm staff. Please do not bring food from home for the animals. You will also see the vegetable fields and the new chicken house. Lunch will be available near the [18]apple barn[/18], where you can buy soup, rolls and homemade cake. There is also a small shop selling eggs and honey. Entrance to the farm is free, but the guided walk costs [19]three[/19] pounds for each person over five years old. All money from the walks will help repair the old farm gate. There is no cash machine at the farm, so please bring money with you if you want to buy lunch or local food. Dogs are not allowed, except guide dogs, because they may frighten the animals. Baby changing facilities are available beside the main yard.  A first aid volunteer will be near the information table throughout the day.

questions.explanation: The speaker says Mrs Carter will meet visitors.
The speaker says the first guided walk begins at ten thirty.
The speaker says visitors should bring a raincoat.
The speaker says children will be allowed to feed the goats.
The speaker says lunch will be available near the apple barn.
The speaker says the guided walk costs three pounds.
